title: गोपाल विंशतिका
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गोपाल विंशतिका ॥

वन्दे गोपालबालं मणिमयतिलकं दिव्यरूपं मुरारिम्।
कृष्णं वृन्दावनेशं नवघनरुचिरं नन्दगोपप्रियं तम्॥ १॥

गोपालं गोकुलेशं कमलदलनयनं कुण्डलाभ्यां विभुषम्।
वंशीवादनलोलं मुरलिमधुरगं श्यामसुन्दरमीडे॥ २॥

नन्दयशोदासुखकरं गोकुलानन्दवर्धनम्।
बालगोपालमीशानं बाललीलाविनोदिनम्॥ ३॥

माखनभक्षणतत्परं दधिचौर्यकलावतम्।
उलूखलबद्धबालं रज्जुबन्धनमोचकम्॥ ४॥

यमलार्जुनभञ्जनं गोपबालकसंयुतम्।
वत्सासुरवधं कृत्वा बकासुरविनाशनम्॥ ५॥

गोचारणं वनचरं गोपगोपीसमावृतम्।
कालियनागदमनं गोवर्धनधरं हरिम्॥ ६॥

इन्द्रमानभञ्जनकरं गोवर्धनगिरिं दधत्।
सप्तदिनानि संधार्य गोकुलं रक्षितं प्रभुम्॥ ७॥

रासलीलाविलासिनं गोपीवस्त्रापहारकम्।
वृन्दावनविहारिणं यमुनातटविक्रमम्॥ ८॥

ब्रह्ममोहनलीलाकृत् गोपालं ब्रह्मवन्दितम्।
धेनुकारिष्टसंहारं प्रलम्बासुरमर्दनम्॥ ९॥

दावाग्निपानं कुर्वाणं गोपरक्षणतत्परम्।
वृषभासुरनाशं च केशीदैत्यविमर्दनम्॥ १०॥

अक्रूरसन्दर्शनकृत् मथुरागमनोत्सुकम्।
कुब्जोद्धारकरं कृष्णं कंसवधकरं प्रभुम्॥ ११॥

उग्रसेनाभिषेककृत् सान्दीपनिगुरोः प्रियम्।
सुदामाबन्धुमव्यक्तं गोपालं गुरुभक्तिमत्॥ १२॥

द्वारकानाथमीशानं रुक्मिणीहरणं शुभम्।
अष्टमहिषीपतिं कृष्णं षोडशसहस्रपम्॥ १३

पार्थसारथिमक्षय्यं गीतोपदेशकं गुरुम्।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय कुरुक्षेत्रे समागतम्॥ १४॥

सुदर्शनचक्रधारिणं भीष्मशरशय्यासमीपगम्।
धर्मराजसभासेव्यं विदुरप्रेमपात्रकम्॥ १५॥

द्रौपदीवस्त्रदानकृत् भक्तरक्षणतत्परम्।
गजेन्द्रमोक्षकर्तारं प्रह्लादवरदं हरिम्॥ १६॥

सर्वधर्मान् परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
अहं त्वां सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥ १७॥

नमो ब्रह्मण्यदेवाय गोब्राह्मणहिताय च।
जगद्धिताय कृष्णाय गोविन्दाय नमो नमः॥ १८॥

सच्चिदानन्दरूपाय कृष्णायाक्लिष्टकारिणे।
नमो वेदान्तवेद्याय गुरवे बुद्धिसाक्षिणे॥ १९॥

वासुदेवसुतं देवं कंसचाणूरमर्दनम्।
देवकीपरमानन्दं कृष्णं वन्दे जगद्गुरुम्॥ २०॥

गोपालविंशतिकां यः पठेद् भक्तिसंयुतः।
कृष्णकृपां समासाद्य गोलोकधामगामिनः॥

॥ इति श्री गोपाल विंशतिका सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gopala Vimshatika ||

Vande Gopalabalam Manimayatilakam Divyarupam Murarim |
Krishnam Vrindavanesham Navaghanaruchram Nandagoppriyam Tam || 1 ||

Gopalam Gokulesesham Kamaladanalayanam Kundalabhyam Vibhusham |
Vamshivadanalolam Muralimadhurgam Shyamasundramide || 2 ||

[...continues through 20 verses...]

Gopalavimshatikam Yah Pathed Bhaktisanyutah |
Krishnakripam Samasadya Golokadhaamagaaminah ||

|| Iti Shri Gopala Vimshatika Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
बालगोपाल, मणिमय तिलकधारी, दिव्यरूप मुरारि, वृन्दावनेश कृष्ण को वन्दन।

माखनचोर, दधिचौर, यमलार्जुन भंजक, कालियनागदमन, गोवर्धनधर।

रासलीला विलासी, ब्रह्ममोहन लीलाकारी, कंसवधकर, मथुरापुरवासी।

द्वारकानाथ, पार्थसारथि, गीतोपदेशक, धर्मसंस्थापक।

सर्वधर्म त्याग कर मेरी शरण में आओ — वासुदेवसुत जगद्गुरु कृष्ण को वन्दना।

भक्ति से पढ़ने वाला कृष्णकृपा से गोलोक धाम प्राप्त करता है।
benefits: Twenty verses covering Krishna's complete divine leela
From Bal-Gopal to Dwarkadhish to Gita Guru
Invokes goloka dham (supreme abode) to devoted readers
Includes the supreme surrender verse from Bhagavad Gita
Ideal for Janmashtami, Gopashtami, and daily worship
description: Gopala Vimshatika (गोपाल विंशतिका) is a sacred prayer dedicated to Lord Krishna. Attributed to Garga Samhita / Bhagavata Tradition, this prayer holds a special place in Hindu devotional literature. Regular recitation is believed to twenty verses covering Krishna's complete divine leela, from Bal-Gopal to Dwarkadhish to Gita Guru, and include the supreme surrender verse from Bhagavad Gita. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami, Gopashtami, and Holi. The text contains 20 verses and takes around 8 minutes to recite, fitting conveniently into a daily spiritual routine. On this page, you can read the complete Gopala Vimshatika in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Gopala Vimshatika?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopala Vimshatika (गोपाल विंशतिका — literally Twenty Verses on Gopala) is a Sanskrit stotra of twenty verses (vimshatika = collection of twenty) narrating the complete divine biography of Sri Krishna from his Bal-Gopala childhood in Gokul-Vrindavana through his Mathura kingship and Dwarka-natha years to his Pandava-sarathya (charioteer of Arjuna) and Bhagavad Gita teaching at Kurukshetra. Each verse focuses on one celebrated leela — the slaying of demons (Putana, Trinavarta, Aghasura, Bakasura, Vatsasura, Pralambasura, Dhenukasura, Keshi, Vrishabhasura, Kamsa, Chanura, Kalayavana, Shishupala), the protection episodes (lifting Govardhana, taming Kaliya, swallowing the forest fire), the lila episodes (Yamala-Arjuna, Putna-vadha, Maakhan-chori, Vastra-harana, Rasa-lila, Brahma-mohana), and the cosmic acts (Kuru-kshetra Gita-upadesha, Draupadi-vastra-dana, Gajendra-moksha). The phala-shruti promises Goloka-dhama-prapti to sincere reciters. (Source: Bhagavata-Krishna stotra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Which Krishna leelas are narrated in the Gopala Vimshatika?
seoFaqs.answer: The twenty verses of the Gopala Vimshatika compress Krishna's complete avatara into a single recitation. The narrative arc covers: (1-3) Bal-Gopala in Gokula — the manimaya-tilaka-clad cowherd child, the joy of Nanda-Yashoda, the bala-leela of childhood pranks; (4-5) Maakhan-chori (butter-stealing), the ulukhala-bandhana (mother tying him to the mortar), the Yamala-Arjuna uprooting; (6-7) Demon-slaying — Vatsasura, Bakasura — and Govardhana-dharana, the Govardhana-uddharana to humble Indra's pride; (8-9) Rasa-lila and Vastra-harana, the Brahma-mohana, Dhenukasura-Pralamba-Keshi-Vrishabha demon-killing; (10-11) the Akrura-Mathura-yatra, Kubja-uddhara, Kamsa-vadha; (12-13) Sandipani-guru-bhakti, Sudama-friendship, Dwarka-natha-rajya, Rukmini-harana, Ashta-mahishi-pati, Shodasha-sahasra-patni; (14-15) Partha-sarathya, Bhagavad Gita upadesha, Bhishma-shara-shayya, Vidura-prema; (16) Draupadi-vastra-dana, Gajendra-moksha, Prahlada-varada; (17) the Charama Shloka of total surrender; (18-20) the cosmic praise as Brahmanya-deva, Sat-chid-ananda-rupa, Vasudeva-suta Jagad-guru. (Source: Gopala Vimshatika; Bhagavata Purana.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Gopala Vimshatika be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Gopala Vimshatika is most powerful on Janmashtami (Bhadrapada Krishna Ashtami — Krishna's birth, the supreme festival), Gopashtami (Kartik Shukla Ashtami — the day Krishna first took the cows out to pasture as a young Gopala, marking the transition from bala-leela to gopa-leela), Radha Ashtami (Bhadrapada Shukla Ashtami — Radha's birthday), Sharad Purnima (Maha-rasa night), Holi / Dol Purnima, Vaikuntha Ekadashi, Gita Jayanti, and on Wednesday (Budhvar) — the day sacred to Krishna. Daily morning recitation facing a Bal-Gopala or Madhava image is the standard household practice across Pushtimargi, Gaudiya, Nimbarka, Vallabha and Madhva sampradayas. The stotra is also recited at the consecration of a Krishna deity, during Gokulashtami Bhagavata-saptaha, on the birth or naming ceremony of a child, and as part of the daily nitya-puja by Krishna upasakas. (Source: Krishna upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What does Vande Gopala Balam mean — the opening verse?
seoFaqs.answer: The opening verse Vande Gopala-balam mani-maya-tilakam divya-rupam Murarim, Krishnam Vrindavanesham nava-ghana-ruchiram Nanda-gopa-priyam tam is a complete Krishna-dhyana in two lines. Word-by-word: Vande — I bow to, Gopala-balam — the cowherd-child, mani-maya-tilakam — wearing a jewelled tilaka on his forehead, divya-rupam — of divine form, Murarim — slayer of the demon Mura; Krishnam — Krishna, Vrindavanesham — Lord of Vrindavana, nava-ghana-ruchiram — radiant as a fresh rain-cloud (the famous nila-megha-shyama complexion), Nanda-gopa-priyam tam — beloved of the cowherd Nanda. The verse establishes the iconographic standard of Bal-Gopala — the divine cowherd-child of Vrindavana, foster-son of Nanda, who slew the demon Mura, dark as a monsoon cloud — and forms the dhyana-base for the entire twenty-verse meditation that follows. (Source: Gopala Vimshatika verse 1.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Gopala Vimshatika grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti of the Gopala Vimshatika promises the following blessings to sincere daily recitation: (1) Krishna-kripa samasadya Goloka-dhama-gaminah — by Krishna's grace the reciter attains Goloka, the supreme abode of Krishna beyond Vaikuntha, (2) the merit of meditating on all of Krishna's twenty principal leelas in a single session — equivalent to a complete Bhagavata-saptaha in compact form, (3) the inclusion of the Charama Shloka (Sarva-dharman parityajya, 18.66) in verse 17 grants the protection of complete sharanagati (total surrender), (4) protection from all demonic and asuric influences (since the stotra repeatedly invokes Krishna's demon-slaying — Putana-mardana, Kaliya-damana, Kamsa-vadha, etc.), (5) blessings for children (since it begins with Bal-Gopala leelas — auspicious for childbirth and early childhood samskaras), (6) prosperity, marital harmony (Krishna as Ashta-mahishi-pati and ideal grihastha) and victory through the Pandava-sarathi-Krishna leelas, (7) liberation through bhakti-yoga as the Vasudeva-suta Jagad-guru. (Source: Gopala Vimshatika phala-shruti.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between the Gopala Vimshatika and the Krishna Karnamrita?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are short Sanskrit Krishna stotras compressing the Lord's leelas into compact verse, but they differ in author, length, structure and emphasis. The Krishna Karnamrita is a 100-110 verse devotional masterpiece by Bilvamangala Thakura (Lilashuka, c. 13th century South India) — a continuous poetic outpouring of madhurya-rasa Krishna-prema in elaborate Sanskrit metres, focused almost entirely on the Vrindavana lila and Krishna's beauty, with little narrative structure. The Gopala Vimshatika is a much shorter 20-verse compendium that systematically narrates Krishna's complete biography — Bal-Gopala (verses 1-3), Mathura demons (4-9), Govardhana-Rasa (6-9), Mathura-Dwarka (10-13), Kurukshetra-Sarathi (14-15), bhakta-rakshana (16), Charama-shloka surrender (17), and cosmic praise (18-20). Krishna Karnamrita is a poetic-rasa text for sahridaya rasikas; Gopala Vimshatika is a biographical-devotional path for daily nitya-parayana. Both are recited in major Vaishnava sampradayas. (Source: Vaishnava stotra-corpus tradition.)
